name: UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following
description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the file is a symbolic link that resolves to a target outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.
extended_description: A product that allows UNIX symbolic links (symlink) as part of paths whether in internal code or through user input can allow an attacker to spoof the symbolic link and traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files. The symbolic link can permit an attacker to read/write/corrupt a file that they originally did not have permissions to access.

parents.name: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')

peers.name: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers
Symbolic Name not Mapping to Correct Object
Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

mapping.rationale: This is a well-known Composite of multiple weaknesses that must all occur simultaneously, although it is attack-oriented in nature.

mechanism.root_cause_summary: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the file is a symbolic link that resolves to a target outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause...
typical_preconditions: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
typical_sources: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
negative_nonfix_signals: style refactor without changing the sensitive path

guard_patterns: same logical object and same sink context

recommended_guard_templates: same logical object and same sink context

stage1.goal: Classify patch chunks by how directly they repair the CWE root cause.
must_focus: direct change to vulnerable sink or validation site
changes that alter boundary, lifecycle, policy, or authorization relation
supporting helper or error-propagation logic needed by the main fix
must_avoid: generic logging changes without security semantics
refactors unrelated to the sensitive sink/path

stage2.goal: Construct discriminative root cause, anchor, predicates, and guards.
must_focus: anchor at the sink or dominant validation block
prefer predicates that encode relations, not lone generic tokens
choose function-local or anchor-window scope whenever possible
must_avoid: predicates that rely on generic names alone
anchors in wrappers that lack the core vulnerability mechanism
recommended_queries: same logical object and same sink context

stage3.goal: Verify whether the target tag still exhibits the vulnerable relation or already contains the fix relation.
must_focus: whether the sensitive sink/path still exists
whether the fix relation is locally present in the same code region
whether anchor relocation is required before concluding absence
must_avoid: declaring NOT_AFFECTED from generic token absence alone
using broad token matches as final evidence

observed_examples.description: Some versions of Perl follow symbolic links when running with the -e option, which allows local users to overwrite arbitrary files via a symlink attack.
Text editor follows symbolic links when creating a rescue copy during an abnormal exit, which allows local users to overwrite the files of other users.
Antivirus update allows local users to create or append to arbitrary files via a symlink attack on a logfile.
Symlink attack allows local users to overwrite files.
